# Internal Normalized YAML Rule Schema

**Purpose:** Defines the canonical rule format for `rules/patterns.yaml` — the file generated by `build.rs` at compile time from vendored upstream sources (gitleaks, secrets-patterns-db, nosey-parker) and hand-authored patterns.

**Predecessor:** `rules/sensitive-patterns.yaml` (6-field schema: `id`, `name`, `category`, `severity`, `regex`, `description`). That file is replaced by `rules/patterns.yaml` under this schema.

**Who generates this file:** `build.rs` at compile time. Do not edit `rules/patterns.yaml` manually — edit vendor sources or hand-authored entries in `rules/vendor/`.

---

## Required Fields

All five fields below must be present on every rule. Build.rs must reject any normalized rule missing any of these.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | string | unique slug across all sources; namespace-prefixed by source: `np.*` (nosey-parker), `gl.*` (gitleaks), `spdb.*` (secrets-patterns-db), `ha.*` (hand-authored) |
| `name` | string | human-readable name, e.g. `AWS API Key` |
| `category` | enum | `secret` \| `pii` \| `infra` |
| `subcategory` | string | fine-grained type within category; examples: `aws`, `github`, `gitlab`, `stripe`, `openai`, `anthropic`, `slack`, `jwt`, `private-key`, `db-conn`, `url-cred`, `sendgrid`, `npm`, `pypi`, `telegram`, `generic` (secret); `email`, `phone`, `ssn`, `cc`, `iban`, `uk-nin`, `pl-pesel`, `fr-insee`, `de-taxid`, `address` (pii); `ipv4`, `uuid` (infra) |
| `regex` | string | RE2-compatible pattern; multiline verbose-mode patterns use YAML block scalar (`\|`) |

---

## Optional Fields

All nine fields below are optional. Defaults are applied by build.rs during normalization when a field is absent from the upstream source.

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `confidence` | enum | `medium` | `high` \| `medium` \| `low` — detection confidence level; see rationale below |
| `entropy` | float \| null | `null` | minimum Shannon entropy of the matched string; `null` means disabled — do NOT default to `0.0`; Rust type: `Option<f64>` |
| `keywords` | string[] | `[]` | lowercase substring pre-filter; if non-empty, request body must contain at least one keyword before the regex is tried; empty list means run regex unconditionally |
| `tags` | string[] | `[]` | capability flags: `fuzzy`, `context-anchored`, `prefix-anchored`, `luhn-check`, `re2-compat`, `hand-authored` |
| `checksum_type` | enum \| null | `null` | `luhn` — post-match checksum validation; only credit card patterns use this currently |
| `replacement_type` | enum | derived from category+subcategory | determines fake data strategy; see [REPLACEMENT-TYPES.md](./REPLACEMENT-TYPES.md) for all enum values and derivation rules |
| `description` | string | `""` | optional human description of what the pattern detects |
| `severity` | enum | derived from confidence | `high` \| `medium` \| `low` — `high` when confidence=`high`, otherwise `medium`; kept for backward compatibility with `src/regex_patterns.rs` |
| `source` | string | required (internal) | `nosey-parker` \| `gitleaks` \| `secrets-patterns-db` \| `hand-authored`; always populated; listed here because it has no upstream equivalent for patterns derived from normalized builds |

---

## Field Rationale

**Why `subcategory` is required (not optional)**

`subcategory` drives `replacement_type` assignment and is always knowable at curation time. Every rule in the curated manifest (`docs/analysis/CURATED-MANIFEST.md`) has been assigned a category and subcategory per decision D-09. There is no valid normalized rule without a known subcategory.

**Why `confidence` defaults to `medium` not `high`**

Patterns with `low` confidence are excluded at curation time — the curated manifest only includes `medium` and `high` confidence patterns. Ungrouped gitleaks patterns without explicit confidence signals get `medium` as a conservative default. Defaulting to `high` would overstate detection quality for patterns that have not been explicitly validated as high-precision.

**Why `entropy: null` is not the same as `entropy: 0.0`**

`null` means the entropy check is disabled entirely — the detection engine skips the Shannon entropy filter for this rule. `0.0` would mean "require entropy > 0.0" which passes almost everything (any non-constant string) and is a meaningless filter. Build.rs must represent this as `Option<f64>` in the Rust struct: `None` = skip filter, `Some(f)` = enforce minimum entropy of `f`.

**Why `keywords: []` means unconditional regex (no pre-filter)**

Empty keyword list is the intended behavior for patterns like PEM private keys where the `-----BEGIN` prefix already makes the regex highly distinctive — adding a keyword filter would be redundant and would just add complexity. Patterns benefit from keywords when the regex is expensive and a cheap substring check can fast-reject most non-matching bodies.

**Why NP `categories` maps to internal `tags` not `category`**

Nosey Parker's `categories` field uses values like `api`, `identifier`, `fuzzy`, `secret` — these are detection capability flags, not taxonomy labels. The internal `category` field uses the two-level taxonomy from decision D-09 (`secret/pii/infra`). Copying NP categories into the internal `category` field would produce invalid enum values. NP categories are preserved as `tags`.

**Why `severity` is retained**

The current Rust struct `RegexPatternRule` in `src/regex_patterns.rs` has a `severity: String` field. Removing it would require a v1.0 code change, which is out of scope. Build.rs derives `severity` from `confidence` if the field is absent from the upstream source. The field is kept optional in the schema to avoid redundancy with `confidence`.

**Why `source` is always populated**

secrets-patterns-db is licensed CC-BY 4.0 — attribution is required. `source: secrets-patterns-db` in every normalized rule from that source enables build.rs to emit attribution notices at build time (embedded binary comment or `LICENSE-NOTICES` file). The field also enables source-specific normalization logic in build.rs (e.g., apply different confidence inference rules for gitleaks vs nosey-parker).

---

## Annotated YAML Example

Complete rule showing every field. Uses the AWS API Key (`np.aws.1`) as the canonical example.

```yaml
# rules/patterns.yaml
# generated by build.rs from rules/vendor/ sources
# DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY — see rules/vendor/ for upstream sources

rules:
  - id: np.aws.1
    name: AWS API Key
    category: secret
    subcategory: aws
    source: nosey-parker
    confidence: high
    regex: '\b((?:A3T[A-Z0-9]|AKIA|AGPA|AIDA|AROA|AIPA|ANPA|ANVA|ASIA)[A-Z0-9]{16})\b'
    keywords: ["akia", "asia", "agpa"]
    entropy: null
    tags: ["api", "identifier", "prefix-anchored"]
    checksum_type: null
    replacement_type: faker_aws_key
    description: "AWS IAM access key ID"
    severity: high
```

---

## Hand-Authored Patterns

Five patterns must be hand-authored because they are absent from all four vendored sources (gitleaks, secrets-patterns-db, nosey-parker, detect-secrets). These are added directly to `rules/vendor/hand-authored/` and normalized into `rules/patterns.yaml` by build.rs.

All hand-authored patterns get:
- `source: hand-authored`
- `tags: ["hand-authored"]` (at minimum)

| ID | Category | Subcategory | Regex Approach | Confidence | Replacement Type | Notes |
|----|----------|-------------|----------------|------------|-----------------|-------|
| `ha.pii.uk-nin` | pii | uk-nin | `\b[A-CEGHJ-PR-TW-Z]{1}[A-CEGHJ-NPR-TW-Z]{1}[0-9]{6}[A-D]{1}\b` | `low` | `generic_random` | format-matching only; no check-digit validation in RE2 |
| `ha.pii.pl-pesel` | pii | pl-pesel | 11-digit format with birth date encoding | `low` | `generic_random` | PESEL has check-digit algorithm not expressible in RE2; conservative format match only |
| `ha.pii.fr-insee` | pii | fr-insee | 15-digit French national ID (Luhn variant) | `low` | `generic_random` | INSEE check digit is a Luhn variant; RE2 cannot validate; format match only |
| `ha.pii.de-taxid` | pii | de-taxid | 11-digit German Pruefzahl format | `low` | `generic_random` | German Steueridentifikationsnummer; format match only; check-digit not RE2-expressible |
| `ha.secret.ibm-cloud-iam` | secret | generic | keyword-anchored with `\b` boundary | `low` | `faker_api_key` | IBM Cloud IAM token format stability unverified; treat as generic API key until confirmed |

**EU PII pattern approach:** All four EU PII patterns use conservative format-matching only. Check-digit algorithms (PESEL, INSEE, German Pruefzahl) cannot be expressed in RE2 — do not attempt to validate check digits in the regex. This increases false positives but is preferable to false negatives. All are tagged `confidence: low` to signal that downstream validation (Layer 3 NER) may be needed.

**IBM Cloud IAM:** The 44-char alphanumeric IBM IAM token format may vary depending on IAM version (could be JWT or opaque string). Tagged `confidence: low` and `replacement_type: faker_api_key` until the format is verified against current IBM Cloud docs.

---

## Defaults Reference Table

Quick reference for build.rs constants. When a field is absent from an upstream rule, apply these defaults.

| Field | Default | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| `confidence` | `medium` | do not default to `high` |
| `entropy` | `null` | `Option<f64> = None` in Rust; do NOT use `0.0` |
| `keywords` | `[]` | empty = no pre-filter |
| `tags` | `[]` | empty = no capability flags |
| `checksum_type` | `null` | `None` in Rust |
| `replacement_type` | derived | see REPLACEMENT-TYPES.md derivation rules |
| `description` | `""` | empty string |
| `severity` | derived from confidence | `high` if confidence=`high`, else `medium` |
| `source` | required | must be populated by build.rs based on which vendor file the rule came from |

---

## Open Questions

1. **Hand-authored EU PII regex correctness** — Resolved direction: use conservative format-matching regex only, no check-digit validation in RE2. Tag as `confidence: low`. Document known FP risk. Actual regex review needed before v1.1 ship.

2. **IBM Cloud IAM token format stability** — Resolved direction: treat as `faker_api_key` with `confidence: low` until verified against current IBM Cloud IAM docs. Token format may be JWT or opaque string depending on IAM version.

3. **`severity` field retention** — Resolved: keep as explicit optional field. Build.rs derives it from `confidence` if absent. Preserves backward compatibility with `src/regex_patterns.rs` `severity: String` field through v1.0.
